query: If I had a wind asset in ERCOT West and a solar asset with the same capacity in CAISO SP15 zone in 2024, which one would make me the most merchant revenue assuming real time market prices only and no curtailment in both regions. Use revenue numbers in $/MWh for the comparison
final_answer:
**The ERCOT West wind asset would have earned far more merchant revenue in 2024.**

## 2024 Merchant Revenue Comparison (Real-Time Prices, No Curtailment)

| Asset | Location | Realized Revenue | Total Generation | Total Revenue per MW |
|-------|----------|------------------|------------------|---------------------|
| **Wind** | ERCOT West (HB_WEST hub) | **$19.36/MWh** | 3,678 MWh/MW/yr | **$71,204/MW/yr** |
| **Solar** | CAISO SP15 (TH_SP15_GEN-APND hub) | **$6.22/MWh** | 1,897 MWh/MW/yr | **$11,797/MW/yr** |

**Winner: ERCOT West wind by $59,407/MW-year, or roughly 6× the merchant revenue.**

## Methodology & Assumptions

- **Capacity:** Same 1 MW nameplate capacity for both assets.
- **Prices:** Real-time wholesale prices only.
  - ERCOT: HB_WEST 15-minute Settlement Point Prices, averaged to hourly (`ercot_spp_real_time_15_min`, GridStatus).
  - CAISO: SP15 Gen trading hub 5-minute LMPs, averaged to hourly (`caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min`, GridStatus).
- **Generation profiles:** Hourly capacity factors from Renewables.ninja for representative locations:
  - Wind: 32.0°N, 101.0°W (West Texas / ERCOT West).
  - Solar: 33.7°N, 116.2°W (Riverside County / CAISO SP15), fixed tilt 25°, south-facing, 10% system losses.
- **No curtailment:** Generation equals profile × capacity in every hour.
- **Merchant revenue:** Σ(hourly generation in MWh × hourly RT price) / Σ(hourly generation).
- 2024 was a leap year (8,784 hours).

## Why the gap is so large

The realized $/MWh is a **generation-weighted average price**, and the two assets sell at very different times:

- **ERCOT West wind** generates heavily in evening/night hours when West Texas real-time prices are highest. The wind profile has a 41.9% average capacity factor and produces a lot during the 7–9 p.m. price spikes.
- **CAISO SP15 solar** generates almost exclusively in mid-day hours when CAISO real-time prices are often suppressed by solar oversupply. Its average capacity factor is 21.6%, and its output is concentrated in the 8 a.m.–4 p.m. window with the lowest LMPs.

## Sources

- ERCOT real-time prices: GridStatus `ercot_spp_real_time_15_min` dataset, location `HB_WEST`.
- CAISO real-time prices: GridStatus `caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min` dataset, location `TH_SP15_GEN-APND`.
- Wind generation profile: Renewables.ninja API, Vestas V90 2000 turbine, 80 m hub height, lat/lon 32.0, -101.0.
- Solar generation profile: Renewables.ninja API, fixed tilt 25°, azimuth 180°, 10% system losses, lat/lon 33.7, -116.2.

steps.content:
**The ERCOT West wind asset would have earned far more merchant revenue in 2024.**

## 2024 Merchant Revenue Comparison (Real-Time Prices, No Curtailment)

| Asset | Location | Realized Revenue | Total Generation | Total Revenue per MW |
|-------|----------|------------------|------------------|---------------------|
| **Wind** | ERCOT West (HB_WEST hub) | **$19.36/MWh** | 3,678 MWh/MW/yr | **$71,204/MW/yr** |
| **Solar** | CAISO SP15 (TH_SP15_GEN-APND hub) | **$6.22/MWh** | 1,897 MWh/MW/yr | **$11,797/MW/yr** |

**Winner: ERCOT West wind by $59,407/MW-year, or roughly 6× the merchant revenue.**

## Methodology & Assumptions

- **Capacity:** Same 1 MW nameplate capacity for both assets.
- **Prices:** Real-time wholesale prices only.
  - ERCOT: HB_WEST 15-minute Settlement Point Prices, averaged to hourly (`ercot_spp_real_time_15_min`, GridStatus).
  - CAISO: SP15 Gen trading hub 5-minute LMPs, averaged to hourly (`caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min`, GridStatus).
- **Generation profiles:** Hourly capacity factors from Renewables.ninja for representative locations:
  - Wind: 32.0°N, 101.0°W (West Texas / ERCOT West).
  - Solar: 33.7°N, 116.2°W (Riverside County / CAISO SP15), fixed tilt 25°, south-facing, 10% system losses.
- **No curtailment:** Generation equals profile × capacity in every hour.
- **Merchant revenue:** Σ(hourly generation in MWh × hourly RT price) / Σ(hourly generation).
- 2024 was a leap year (8,784 hours).

## Why the gap is so large

The realized $/MWh is a **generation-weighted average price**, and the two assets sell at very different times:

- **ERCOT West wind** generates heavily in evening/night hours when West Texas real-time prices are highest. The wind profile has a 41.9% average capacity factor and produces a lot during the 7–9 p.m. price spikes.
- **CAISO SP15 solar** generates almost exclusively in mid-day hours when CAISO real-time prices are often suppressed by solar oversupply. Its average capacity factor is 21.6%, and its output is concentrated in the 8 a.m.–4 p.m. window with the lowest LMPs.

## Sources

- ERCOT real-time prices: GridStatus `ercot_spp_real_time_15_min` dataset, location `HB_WEST`.
- CAISO real-time prices: GridStatus `caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min` dataset, location `TH_SP15_GEN-APND`.
- Wind generation profile: Renewables.ninja API, Vestas V90 2000 turbine, 80 m hub height, lat/lon 32.0, -101.0.
- Solar generation profile: Renewables.ninja API, fixed tilt 25°, azimuth 180°, 10% system losses, lat/lon 33.7, -116.2.
